summary:
- publisher=Lloyd's List, title=Hormuz crisis at two months: a tale of four shipping markets, directness=high, why=confirms sustained disruption across VLCC, midsize crude, and product tanker markets with no return to normal, next=https://www.lloydslist.com/LL1157052/Hormuz-crisis-at-two-months-a-tale-of-four-shipping-markets  
- publisher=Lloyd's List, title=Strait of Hormuz open, says Iranian foreign minister, directness=medium, why=Iran claims Hormuz is open amid Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, but market data contradicts operational reality, next=https://www.lloydslist.com/LL1156947/Strait-of-Hormuz-open-says-Iranian-foreign-minister  
- publisher=Lloyd's List, title=The Daily View: Don’t mistake a ceasefire for a supply surge, directness=high, why=explicitly warns that even a 30-day reopening won’t immediately restore logistics—backlog and port congestion require months to clear, next=https://www.lloydslist.com/LL1157112/The-Daily-View-Dont-mistake-a-ceasefire-for-a-supply-surge  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Hormuz remains near standstill after ceasefire, directness=high, why=confirms operational closure persists despite diplomatic claims; cites US-Iran negotiations and suspended strikes, next=https://af.reuters.com/world/middle-east/hormuz-remains-near-standstill-after-ceasefire-2026-04-10/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=US-sanctioned Chinese tanker passes Strait of Hormuz despite US blockade, directness=high, why=indicates enforcement gaps and potential circumvention of sanctions; key lead for compliance monitoring, next=http://uk.reuters.com/business/energy/us-sanctioned-chinese-tanker-passes-strait-hormuz-despite-us-blockade-data-shows-2026-04-14/  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Chinese container ships pass through Strait of Hormuz second attempt, directness=medium, why=confirms renewed but risky transit attempts by Chinese vessels; signals evolving risk tolerance, next=https://in.reuters.com/world/china/chinese-container-ships-pass-through-strait-hormuz-second-attempt-data-shows-2026-03-30/  
- publisher=Lloyd's List, title=Dynacom-operated VLCC carrying Saudi crude passes through Strait of Hormuz, directness=low, why=pre-dates crisis (March 16); no relevance to May 2026 status, excluded from actionable leads  
- publisher=Reuters, title=Oil tanker steer clear Hormuz ahead US blockade, directness=medium, why=confirms risk-averse behavior by tankers despite ceasefire; supports conclusion that access remains de facto closed, next=http://uk.reuters.com/business/energy/oil-tankers-steer-clear-hormuz-ahead-us-blockade-2026-04-13/
